eras.name: The Rupp Dynasty
eras.desc: Four national championships. The Baron of the Bluegrass. The man who built Kentucky basketball into a religion.

eras.name: The Hall Years
eras.desc: Following a legend. The 1978 national championship. The burden and the glory of being next.

eras.name: The Sutton Era
eras.desc: Elite Eight runs, NCAA violations, and the scandal that nearly destroyed the program.

eras.name: The Pitino Resurrection
eras.desc: From probation to the 1996 national championship. Full-court press. The Unforgettables. Kentucky reborn.

eras.name: The Tubby Smith Years
eras.desc: A title in year one. Ten straight NCAA tournaments. Never enough for the most demanding fanbase in America.

eras.name: The Gillispie Interlude
eras.desc: Two seasons. No NCAA tournament. The lowest point in modern Kentucky basketball.

eras.name: The Calipari Era
eras.desc: One-and-done factory. The 2012 national championship. 38-0. The most polarizing era in program history.

eras.name: The Pope Era
eras.desc: The former Wildcat returns home. A new beginning built on old roots.

players.name: Dan Issel
players.tagline: Kentucky's all-time leading scorer. The Horse from Batavia who averaged 33.9 points as a senior and left Lexington with every record in the book.

players.name: Pat Riley
players.tagline: Before he became the most iconic coach in NBA history, he was Adolph Rupp's do-everything forward — a kid from Schenectady who learned to win in Lexington.

players.name: Ralph Beard
players.tagline: The heart of the Fabulous Five. Two national championships, an Olympic gold medal, and a career destroyed by the point-shaving scandal.

players.name: Alex Groza
players.tagline: The Fabulous Five's dominant center. Two-time NCAA Tournament MOP, Olympic gold medalist, then banned from the NBA for life.
players.stat: 2x NCAA Champion • 2x NCAA Tournament MOP • 1948 Olympic Gold

players.name: Cliff Hagan
players.tagline: The hook shot artist who led the undefeated 1953-54 team that couldn't play in the NCAA Tournament — then became a five-time NBA All-Star.

players.name: Frank Ramsey
players.tagline: The original sixth man. Kentucky's do-everything guard who became the NBA's first great bench player and won seven championships with the Celtics.

players.name: Cotton Nash
players.tagline: The last great star of the Rupp era before integration. Three-time All-American, two-sport pro, and the man who proved Kentucky could still recruit with anyone.

players.name: Louie Dampier
players.tagline: Rupp's Runts point guard who took Kentucky to the 1966 championship game, then became the ABA's all-time assists and three-point leader.
players.stat: 1,575 pts • ABA All-Time 3PT Leader • 7x ABA All-Star

players.name: Jack Givens
players.tagline: Goose. 41 points in the national championship game. The greatest single-game performance in Kentucky history.
players.stat: 2,038 pts • 1978 NCAA Tournament MOP • 41 pts in title game

players.name: Kyle Macy
players.tagline: The Purdue transfer who became the best free throw shooter in Kentucky history and the steadiest hand Joe B. Hall ever coached.

players.name: Sam Bowie
players.tagline: The most talented center in Kentucky history, remembered forever as the man drafted before Michael Jordan. The full story is more complicated — and more heartbreaking.

players.name: Kenny Walker
players.tagline: Sky Walker. The high-flying forward from Roberta, Georgia who became the SEC Player of the Year and one of the most electric athletes in Kentucky history.
players.stat: 2,080 pts • SEC POY 1986 • Slam Dunk Contest Champion 1989

players.name: Rex Chapman
players.tagline: The King. Kentucky's golden boy who left after two years, played 12 NBA seasons, hit rock bottom, and rebuilt his life one viral tweet at a time.

players.name: Jamal Mashburn
players.tagline: Monster Mash. The Bronx kid who became the centerpiece of Pitino's rebuild, led Kentucky to the Final Four, and turned a 12-year NBA career into a restaurant empire.
players.now: Entrepreneur, restaurant owner (multiple Papa John's, Outback franchises)

players.name: Tony Delk
players.tagline: The 1996 championship was his. Seven three-pointers in the title game. The quiet assassin from Brownsville, Tennessee who delivered Kentucky's sixth national championship.
players.stat: 1,890 pts • 1996 Final Four MOP • 7 threes in title game

players.name: Antoine Walker
players.tagline: Employee Number 8. The Chicago kid who danced at midcourt, won a championship as a sophomore, made $110 million in the NBA — and lost it all.

players.name: Ron Mercer
players.tagline: The most naturally talented player of the Pitino era. Two years, one championship, sixth pick, and a what-if NBA career that never reached its ceiling.

players.name: Derek Anderson
players.tagline: DA. The Ohio State transfer who became the best player on the 1996 championship team, tore his ACL chasing a repeat, and still went 13th in the draft.

players.name: Jeff Sheppard
players.tagline: The walk-on who became the 1998 Final Four MOP. Two championships. The most improbable hero in Kentucky history.
players.stat: 1,290 pts • 2x NCAA Champion • 1998 Final Four MOP

players.name: Tayshaun Prince
players.tagline: The Thin Man. Too skinny, too slow, couldn't create his own shot — until he became a four-year starter, an NBA champion, and one of the best defenders of his generation.
players.now: VP of basketball operations, Memphis Grizzlies

players.name: Rajon Rondo
players.tagline: The most brilliant basketball mind to pass through Lexington. Two years, then the NBA, where he became a four-time All-Star and one of the greatest assist men ever.

players.name: Jodie Meeks
players.tagline: 54 points against Tennessee. The bright spot in Kentucky's darkest chapter — the kid who kept shooting while the program burned around him.
players.stat: 1,303 pts • 54 pts vs Tennessee (school record) • SEC POY 2009

players.name: Patrick Patterson
players.tagline: The bridge between eras. Recruited by Tubby, survived Gillispie, thrived under Calipari — the steady big man who connected Kentucky's past to its future.

players.name: John Wall
players.tagline: Year One of the Calipari era. The fastest player in college basketball, the most exciting freshman since Derrick Rose, and the moment Kentucky announced it was back.

players.name: DeMarcus Cousins
players.tagline: Boogie. The most dominant physical force in college basketball, combustible and unstoppable in equal measure. One year, fifth pick, four All-Star selections, and an Achilles that changed everything.

players.name: Anthony Davis
players.tagline: He was a 6-foot-2 guard going into his junior year of high school. Then he grew eight inches — and became the most dominant college player of his generation.
players.stat: 568 pts • 14.2 ppg • 10.4 rpg • 4.7 bpg • #1 pick • Naismith POY

players.name: Michael Kidd-Gilchrist
players.tagline: MKG. The heart and soul of the 2012 championship team. The kid from Somerdale, New Jersey who played every possession like it was his last.

players.name: Julius Randle
players.tagline: The bull from Dallas. One year, one double-double machine season, seventh pick — then an NBA All-Star who proved one-and-done players can still develop.

players.name: Karl-Anthony Towns
players.tagline: KAT. The cornerstone of the 38-0 team that couldn't finish the job. First pick. Four All-Star selections. The most skilled big man Kentucky has ever produced.
players.stat: 406 pts • 10.3 ppg • 6.7 rpg • #1 pick 2015 • 4x NBA All-Star

players.name: Devin Booker
players.tagline: The son of a pro who came off the bench behind the Harrison twins, averaged 10 points — then became the best player from any of them. 70 points in an NBA game. Four All-Star selections. Still climbing.

players.name: Jamal Murray
players.tagline: The Canadian kid who hit the logo three in the SEC Tournament, went seventh in the draft, then dropped 50 in the NBA playoffs — twice in one series.

players.name: De'Aaron Fox
players.tagline: The fastest player in college basketball — literally. He buried Lonzo Ball in the Sweet Sixteen, went fifth in the draft, and became an All-Star running Sacramento's offense.

players.name: Bam Adebayo
players.tagline: From a single-wide trailer in Pinetown, North Carolina to the NBA All-Star game. One year at Kentucky, then a Heat lifer who became one of the most versatile bigs in the league.

players.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
players.tagline: SGA. The quiet Canadian who barely cracked the top 20 in his recruiting class, started every game as a freshman, went 11th in the draft — and became a top-five player in the NBA.
players.stat: 540 pts • 14.4 ppg • 5.1 apg • NBA All-Star • 2025 MVP candidate

players.name: Tyler Herro
players.tagline: The kid from Milwaukee who decommitted from Wisconsin, bet on himself at Kentucky, then dropped 37 in the Eastern Conference Finals as a 20-year-old rookie.
players.stat: 493 pts • 14.0 ppg • 6th Man of the Year 2022

players.name: Oscar Tshiebwe
players.tagline: The Congolese rebounding machine who transferred from West Virginia, won every national player of the year award, grabbed 15 boards a game — and stayed four years.
players.stat: 1,471 pts • 15.1 rpg • Naismith POY 2022 • Wooden Award 2022

players.name: Reed Sheppard
players.tagline: Kentucky royalty. The son of Jeff and Stacey Reed Sheppard — both 1998 champions — who came home, shot 52% from three, and went third in the 2024 draft.

players.name: Jaland Lowe
players.highSchool: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart HS
